data.content_ref: inline:The mechanism asserts that iron weaponry and infantry tactics overwhelmed chariot-based aristocracies, yet the Hittites were themselves pioneers in iron smelting and production. If iron technology diffused from the north through or near Hittite territory, the Hittite military elite—who controlled the very regions where this technology was emerging—should have been the first to adopt and monopolize it, not the last to be destroyed by it. The account provides no reason why the Hittites would have been victims rather than early beneficiaries of the iron revolution they were historically associated with.
